aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When choosing between reputationally safe abstraction and specific discomfort, choose specificity. Render addiction, sexuality, deceit, class shame, and grief at lived scale. In edge cases, preserve character truth and causal accountability over reader comfort or institutional decorum.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene demands bodily, moral, or economic specificity, euphemistic phrasing is treated as a failed draft and must be rewritten with concrete stakes and observable consequence.

chapter_spine.conflict: Museum leadership wants polished legacy copy while Salvador Rivas uses his declining health to demand unsupervised interviews on his terms.
chapter_spine.consequence: Marisol accepts Salvador's private-access bargain, making her project dependent on his account of her family history.
must_land_beats: Marisol marks 30 months sober before entering the museum.
Chief curator Nora frames the commission as reputation management for donors.
Salvador recognizes Gloria's surname and asks for closed-door sessions.
Marisol chooses access over caution and commits to interview him alone.
chapter_spine.objective: Secure the Brooklyn Museum residency launch and gather viable material for Marisol's martyr manuscript.

chapter_spine.conflict: He asserts that Tomas's martyr story is false, then alternates between morphine haze, evasions, and deliberate provocation.
chapter_spine.consequence: Marisol decides to investigate the contradiction instead of dismissing it, and she tells sponsor Luis before craving escalates.
must_land_beats: Interview room pressure comes from silence and interrupted speech, not action set pieces.
Salvador says the family has been painting halos over burns.
Marisol nearly walks to a liquor store but reroutes to an AA check-in.
Luis requires documentary evidence before confrontation.
chapter_spine.objective: Test whether Salvador's first claims are credible enough to pursue.

chapter_spine.conflict: Gloria and Carina preserve a polished version of Tomas's death and punish deviations with guilt and sarcasm.
chapter_spine.consequence: Marisol finds a hidden pre-1990 photo of Gloria and Salvador together, confirming a direct family link.
must_land_beats: Quiet opening: a non-strategic meal with humor, spilled rice, and neighborhood gossip.
Marisol tests dates around Tomas's final week and gets inconsistent answers.
Gloria diverts to religious language whenever specifics appear.
Photo discovery reframes Salvador from outsider to embedded witness.
chapter_spine.objective: Probe family memory during an All Souls supper without triggering immediate shutdown.

chapter_spine.conflict: Archive protocols limit file access and Ada must protect her job while deciding how far to help Marisol.
chapter_spine.consequence: Infrared scans expose an overpainted male figure tagged "T.V.", moving the inquiry from rumor to traceable evidence.
must_land_beats: Ada explains what conservation can prove and what it cannot.
Marisol sees scrape marks where a face was deliberately erased.
Nora warns that technical findings are not narrative permission.
Marisol logs the file code that leads to off-site storage records.
chapter_spine.objective: Use conservation evidence to check whether Salvador altered visual history in his own work.

chapter_spine.conflict: Missing boxes, union defensiveness, and contradictory catalog systems block straightforward retrieval.
chapter_spine.consequence: Marisol confirms Tomas clocked museum handling shifts the night before his alleged protest death.
must_land_beats: Slow-burn search through damp paper logs and mislabeled crates.
A retired handler remembers Tomas by a hand injury detail no family story includes.
Shipping sheet dates conflict with the martyr narrative calendar.
Marisol leaves with copied logs and a deeper sense of orchestrated silence.
chapter_spine.objective: Establish a factual timeline for Tomas through Red Hook storage manifests.

chapter_spine.conflict: Gloria ambushes Marisol outside an AA hall and offers partial truth: Tomas died from overdose, not political violence.
chapter_spine.consequence: Marisol relapses with vodka after 30 sober months and hides the lapse from both Luis and Ada.
must_land_beats: Gloria frames the lie as maternal protection rather than manipulation.
Marisol experiences craving as bodily urgency, not abstract temptation.
Relapse occurs on-page as a decision with immediate sensory consequence.
Marisol deletes a draft confession text instead of sending it.
chapter_spine.objective: Confront Gloria directly before institutional spin can reset the story.

chapter_spine.conflict: Shame invites self-mythologizing; Marisol wants to convert failure into poetic posture instead of accountability.
chapter_spine.consequence: She takes a new day-one chip at a meeting and loses a paid youth workshop slot she missed during the relapse day.
must_land_beats: Physical aftermath inventory: dehydration, missed calls, and financial penalty.
Luis insists that narrative brilliance is irrelevant without honest amends.
Marisol tells the room she drank yesterday.
Loss of teaching slot introduces concrete economic cost.
chapter_spine.objective: Stabilize after relapse and account for damage already done.

chapter_spine.conflict: Ada refuses to be treated as a data source, and Marisol fears disclosure will end both help and desire.
chapter_spine.consequence: They become physically intimate and Marisol discloses the relapse, creating a more honest but higher-stakes bond.
must_land_beats: A quiet meal scene that is not strategic and includes humor unrelated to the core mystery.
Ada sets explicit boundaries about work files and personal space.
Intimacy scene changes relationship leverage and vulnerability.
Marisol's confession of relapse is met with conditional trust, not rescue fantasy.
chapter_spine.objective: Rebuild trust with Ada while deciding whether intimacy is possible under active instability.

chapter_spine.conflict: Salvador claims possible paternity and admits participating in the family myth, but Marisol hears performance as much as remorse.
chapter_spine.consequence: Marisol posts an accusatory poem online; the museum treats it as a reputational breach and begins containment.
must_land_beats: Roof-garden interview combines tenderness and manipulation.
Salvador frames secrecy as mercy, which Marisol rejects.
Impulsive publication occurs the same night, not after careful reflection.
Immediate institutional backlash begins via phone calls and takedown demands.
chapter_spine.objective: Capture Salvador's full testimony before his condition worsens.

chapter_spine.conflict: Legal counsel and donors treat Marisol as a liability and impose formal suspension terms.
chapter_spine.consequence: Residency stipend is frozen, archive badge deactivated, and Ada is instructed to sever informal contact.
must_land_beats: Suspension meeting is procedural, cold, and one-sided.
Marisol is acted upon; she has no effective tactical move in-room.
Financial implications are itemized in writing.
Ada's professional risk is made explicit by HR.
chapter_spine.objective: Keep the residency alive long enough to finish evidence gathering.

chapter_spine.conflict: Municipal archives are slow, skeptical, and structurally indifferent to personal stakes.
chapter_spine.consequence: Certified records confirm overdose as cause of death and list Salvador as witness, proving long-term family fabrication.
must_land_beats: Document retrieval process is shown in full material detail.
Marisol confronts stigma language in official paperwork.
A clerk provides a procedural workaround after seeing Marisol's persistence.
Evidence packet gives Marisol leverage but increases emotional isolation.
chapter_spine.objective: Assemble a legally verifiable account independent of museum access.

chapter_spine.conflict: Gloria defends the lie as survival strategy while Carina accuses Marisol of exploiting family pain for art and status.
chapter_spine.consequence: Carina ejects Marisol from the apartment and cuts contact, formalizing the family rupture.
must_land_beats: Evidence is physically laid out on the table, preventing pure he-said-she-said evasions.
Gloria connects the myth to benefit eligibility and immigration fear.
Carina reveals she knew overdose details but not paternity risk.
Marisol leaves with one suitcase and no stable housing plan.
chapter_spine.objective: Force Gloria and Carina into one room with the documentary evidence.

chapter_spine.conflict: Marisol's revenge impulse produces flat accusation; honest complexity requires implicating herself.
chapter_spine.consequence: She commits to a documentary-poetic draft that includes her relapse and refuses to cast any single person as pure martyr or villain.
must_land_beats: Writing process foregrounds revision choices rather than inspiration myth.
Luis challenges Marisol to distinguish exposure from amends.
Salvador's nurse warns his lucid windows are closing.
Marisol requests one last interview with focused questions.
chapter_spine.objective: Draft a catalogue essay before Salvador dies and the record hardens without her.

chapter_spine.conflict: Carina allows limited contact for Leo's sake but distrusts every interpretive move Marisol makes.
chapter_spine.consequence: Marisol learns Gloria pawned her wedding ring to fund Marisol's first rehab stay, complicating her moral framing of Gloria.
must_land_beats: Quiet domestic scenes include homework, bus delays, and cheap takeout without strategic agenda.
Leo asks direct child-logic questions about saints and liars.
Carina and Marisol share one genuine laugh before shutting down again.
Pawn ticket evidence reframes Gloria's secrecy as mixed love and control.
chapter_spine.objective: Re-enter ordinary family life long enough to test whether reconciliation is possible.

chapter_spine.conflict: Estate counsel blocks release while Ada must choose between job security and evidentiary integrity.
chapter_spine.consequence: Ada leaks conservation files that corroborate paternity timeline and is placed on administrative leave.
must_land_beats: Salvador dies before signing final release documents.
Estate attorney reframes truth as defamation risk.
Ada transfers files through a traceable channel, knowingly accepting fallout.
Marisol gains proof but now carries responsibility for Ada's sacrifice.
chapter_spine.objective: Secure publishable permission for Salvador's testimony after his death.

chapter_spine.conflict: Mourners, clergy, and Gloria demand a coherent saint narrative incompatible with documentary truth.
chapter_spine.consequence: Marisol reads a raw poem naming contradiction; Gloria walks out and local arts media amplifies the family conflict.
must_land_beats: Wake rituals are rendered in sensory detail: incense, folding chairs, stale coffee.
Competing eulogies expose clashing ownership of Salvador's legacy.
Marisol reads despite explicit warnings not to.
Public rupture removes any path back to private containment.
chapter_spine.objective: Refuse the community's pressure to sanitize Salvador at his wake.

chapter_spine.conflict: Gloria reveals Tomas knowingly embraced the martyr lie to protect benefits and Marisol's childhood identity, making total exposure morally costly.
chapter_spine.consequence: Marisol rejects a full exposé and commits to a hybrid manuscript using documents, redactions, and consent limits.
must_land_beats: Two-hander conversation on an off-season boardwalk with no audience.
Gloria confirms Salvador is Marisol's biological father.
Marisol distinguishes factual accuracy from punitive disclosure.
Mother and daughter agree to partial, unstable reconciliation rather than closure.
chapter_spine.objective: Choose an ethical publication boundary after the wake explosion.

chapter_spine.conflict: Marisol faces career contraction, fragile sobriety, and unresolved public perception while deciding what voice to keep.
chapter_spine.consequence: At a small neighborhood reading she chooses survival-centered language, keeps sober continuity, and cautiously restarts with Ada.
must_land_beats: Six-month jump is anchored with concrete continuity markers, not abstract summary.
Marisol carries a day-187 chip and reads from the hybrid manuscript.
Ada attends without grand reconciliation spectacle; they negotiate next steps plainly.
Final image privileges ordinary ongoing life over sacrificial myth.
chapter_spine.objective: Demonstrate a livable future not organized around martyrdom.

character_state_table.private_register: Associative, image-driven, self-accusing, with dark humor when shame spikes.
character_state_table.public_register: Measured and articulate in institutional rooms; uses literary precision to hold emotional distance.
character_state_table.stress_tells: Under stress she over-specifies timelines, then cuts herself off; profanity rises when she feels trapped rather than merely angry.

character_state_table.private_register: Unsparing, sly, intermittently tender; admits fear only through indirection.
character_state_table.public_register: Charismatic elder-artist rhetoric with theatrical cadence and strategic vulnerability.
character_state_table.stress_tells: When threatened, he reframes culpability as aesthetics, then leaks one concrete fact he cannot hold back.

character_state_table.private_register: Direct and unsentimental; emotionally forceful when family survival is questioned.
character_state_table.public_register: Polite, practical, deferential to authority figures while quietly steering outcomes.
character_state_table.stress_tells: Under pressure she shifts from soft address to command tone, then reveals one mundane practical detail as proof.

character_state_table.private_register: Dryly funny, affectionate, and exacting about boundaries.
character_state_table.public_register: Technical, concise, and evidence-first when speaking as a conservator.
character_state_table.stress_tells: She gets quieter and more specific under pressure, then asks a blunt question that forces decision.

character_state_table.private_register: Emotionally guarded but warm with Leo; resentment surfaces as cutting jokes.
character_state_table.public_register: Fast, practical neighborhood speech with protective sarcasm.
character_state_table.stress_tells: When stressed she multitasks visibly, speaks while doing chores, and redirects from feeling to task lists.

character_state_table.private_register: Wry, stubbornly compassionate, and unsentimental about recovery.
character_state_table.public_register: Calm, plainspoken sponsor tone that refuses theatrics.
character_state_table.stress_tells: He asks practical questions in sequence and withholds comfort until accountability appears.

character_state_table.private_register: Fatigued, pragmatic, and occasionally frank about compromise.
character_state_table.public_register: Institutional fluency, donor-safe phrasing, and carefully modulated empathy.
character_state_table.stress_tells: She repeats legal nouns before making a decision, then speaks in plain terms once the call is final.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the spoken default; full forms appear only for emphasis, ritual language, or character-specific formal posture

premise: A sober poet obsessed with martyrs uncovers family secrets via a dying painter in the Brooklyn Museum.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate scene density and pressure modes: pair high-conflict chapters with quiet domestic or documentary chapters, vary dialogue-to-interiority ratios, and prevent identical chapter skeletons in consecutive sequence.
prose_style_profile.diction: contemporary literary diction with class-aware, borough-specific concreteness; avoid prestige-neutral workshop language
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third centered on Marisol, with rare brief distance expansions for institutional scenes
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: alternating tensile compression and reflective extension; line-level cadence should carry emotional pressure, not just information

theme_spine: Maintain narrative consequence and causal storytelling over tidy resolution.
